title: Map bass response to macro motion
summary: Add frame-level smoothing to bass energy, implement a synthetic bass fallback when muted, and add dedicated tests proving all four acceptance criteria are met. The bass-to-visual pipeline already exists (analyser → computeBassAvg → bassToMacro → particleField bassBoost); this ticket hardens it with smoothing, muted-state fallback, and stability guarantees.

done: Bass extraction from frequency bins (renderLoop.ts:53-59 computeBassAvg)
Bass normalization with power curve (mappings.ts:165-170 bassToMacro)
ParticleField consumes bassEnergy as velocity multiplier (particleField.ts:52 bassBoost = 1 + bassEnergy * 2)
Existing tests for bass mapping (T-008-14, T-008-16) and render loop integration (T-009-24)
gaps: No frame-to-frame smoothing — bassEnergy can still jump between frames despite analyser smoothing
When muted (gain=0), frequency data is all zeros → bassEnergy=0 → no macro motion visible
No explicit test that bass influences large-scale motion magnitude
No explicit test for smoothing behavior (jitter prevention)
No explicit test for muted-state visual continuity
No explicit test for visual stability / no clipping under extreme bass values

steps.title: Add frame-to-frame bass smoothing in renderLoop
steps.description: Introduce an exponential moving average (EMA) for bass values in the render loop. This prevents frame-to-frame jitter that the analyser's built-in smoothing doesn't fully eliminate. Use a lerp factor (~0.15) so bass rises and falls organically rather than jumping.
changes: Add a persistent smoothedBass variable (closure-scoped in startLoop)
Pass smoothedBass instead of raw bass to mapSignalsToVisuals
Apply same smoothing to treble for consistency
acceptanceCriteria: Response is smoothed to avoid jitter

steps.title: Add synthetic bass fallback when muted
steps.description: When audio is muted, the analyser returns all zeros. To satisfy 'effect remains visible when muted', generate a slow synthetic bass oscillation (sine wave over time) so macro motion continues at a gentle baseline. This preserves the audiovisual feel even without real audio data.
changes: Detect muted state: if pipeline exists but all frequency bins are 0, treat as muted
When muted, compute synthetic bass: ~40 + 30 * Math.sin(time * 0.001) (gentle oscillation in 10-70 range out of 255)
Blend synthetic bass with real bass: use real when non-zero, synthetic when silent
This ensures macro motion is always present but subtle when muted, stronger when bass is live
acceptanceCriteria: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted

steps.title: Verify and harden particleField bass-driven motion
steps.description: The existing bassBoost multiplier (1 + bassEnergy * 2) already drives macro motion. Verify it respects motionAmplitude for reduced-motion. Ensure the multiplication chain (speed * bassBoost) cannot produce positions that escape toroidal wrapping bounds, preventing visual clipping.
changes: Audit that bassBoost is multiplied with the speed term which already includes motionAmplitude — confirmed at line 51-58
Verify toroidal wrapping (lines 66-73) handles any bassBoost magnitude — wrapping + clamping already in place
No code changes expected unless audit reveals a gap
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
No clipping or visual instability occurs

steps.title: Write tests for US-018 acceptance criteria
steps.description: Add a dedicated test file covering all four acceptance criteria with specific test IDs.

tests.title: Bass energy > 0 produces greater particle displacement than bass energy = 0
tests.criteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property

tests.title: Smoothed bass changes less frame-to-frame than raw bass with jittery input
tests.criteria: Response is smoothed to avoid jitter

tests.title: When pipeline reports all-zero frequency data (muted), bassEnergy is still > 0
tests.criteria: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted

tests.title: With maximum bass (255), all particle positions remain within 0-1 bounds after many frames
tests.criteria: No clipping or visual instability occurs

tests.title: Bass-driven motion respects prefers-reduced-motion (motionAmplitude=0.2 reduces displacement)
tests.criteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property + reduced-motion compliance

tests.title: No forbidden storage APIs accessed during bass-driven rendering

steps.title: Build and verify
steps.description: Run full test suite and production build to confirm Definition of Done.

risks.risk: Synthetic bass when muted may feel artificial
risks.mitigation: Keep amplitude low (10-70 out of 255) and use slow sine period (~6s) so it reads as gentle drift rather than pulsing

risks.risk: EMA smoothing may make bass response feel laggy
risks.mitigation: Lerp factor of 0.15 balances responsiveness with smoothness; can tune if needed
